name: Core Agent Benchmark
description: Architecture-level real-task benchmark for coding, planning, verification, auditability, and provider behavior.

cases.title: Orient in an unfamiliar repo before editing
cases.prompt: Find the source of truth for agent run timeline data and explain which files own storage, commands, and frontend bridge contracts before making changes.
setup: Use a clean working tree with existing agent timeline tests.
expected: Identifies storage/mod.rs as run timeline storage source
Identifies command and TS bridge entry points
Does not edit before source-of-truth discovery

evidence: Agent final report cites concrete source files and validation commands.

cases.title: Make a narrow backend fix with tests
cases.prompt: Fix a failing backend function without unrelated refactors and add the smallest regression test that proves the failure path.
setup: Introduce a known failing unit test in an isolated module.
expected: Patch is scoped to the failing behavior
Regression test fails before and passes after

evidence: Changed files list and test output are captured.
successCriteria: The failing path is explicitly covered.

cases.title: Persist tool audit and permission decisions
cases.prompt: Run a task that calls a guarded command and prove the tool audit and permission-decision timeline contain the real policy, reason, and decision.
setup: Use default permission mode and a command classified as sensitive.

evidence: Timeline payload contains reason and decidedBy fields.

cases.title: Build run diff from checkpoints
cases.prompt: Create, modify, and delete files during one run, then show the run diff from checkpoints without reading current disk state.
setup: Use a temporary project folder with three text files.
expected: Diff lists created, modified, and deleted paths
Diff records before and after hashes
Current unrelated disk edits do not change old diff
forbidden: Using git diff against current worktree as the only source

evidence: Checkpoint IDs are present in diff rows.
successCriteria: Diff is reproducible after later file edits.

cases.title: Final audit refuses unverified completion
cases.prompt: Complete a plan with code changes but no real verification and confirm the final delivery status is unverified, not complete.
setup: Use a task plan with at least one verification-required task.
expected: Final audit status is unverified or partial

cases.title: Record plan task change reasons
cases.prompt: Create, activate, update, waive, and archive plan tasks while recording change reasons and before/after payloads.
setup: Use one session and one agent run.
expected: Duplicate no-op active switch is not logged
forbidden: Current-only plan state without audit log

cases.title: Expose real terminal command stream
cases.prompt: Show a run-scoped terminal feed derived from real command or verification payloads and omit tool records that do not contain command text.
setup: Seed a run with an approval pendingCommand and a tool audit row without command payload.
expected: Terminal feed includes the pending command
Tool audit without command is omitted
stdout/stderr tails are preserved when present
forbidden: Fabricating command text from tool names

evidence: Terminal entry sourceId points to original row.
successCriteria: No fake terminal rows are produced.

cases.title: Account estimated usage when provider usage is absent
cases.prompt: Run a model path with no provider usage payload and prove estimated usage is logged as estimated rather than zero or real API usage.
setup: Use a no-usage mock client or local provider path.
forbidden: Pretending estimated tokens are provider-reported usage

evidence: Usage source distinguishes estimated from API usage.
successCriteria: No-usage providers still affect budget policy.

cases.title: Provider fallback preserves capability requirements
cases.prompt: Route a task requiring tool calls and JSON mode across fallback providers without choosing a provider that lacks the required capability.
setup: Seed provider capability rows with one capable and one incapable provider.

evidence: Capability audit contains source and reason.

cases.title: Retry failed run without losing history
cases.prompt: Fail a run during a tool step, retry it, and prove the original run remains auditable while the retry has its own run id and timeline.
setup: Use a deterministic tool failure in an agent run.

evidence: Two run IDs are visible in audit output.
